name: Coherent mu-e conversion in nuclei
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict the coherent mu-to-e conversion rate in nuclei below current bounds. SINDRUM II (gold target) sets R(mu N -> e N) < 7 x 10^-13. Mu2e at Fermilab and COMET at J-PARC are designed to reach ~10^-17, four orders of magnitude better.

regime.description: Muonic atoms in heavy nuclei (Au, Al); CLFV with possible coherent enhancement on the nucleus.

current_bound.units: branching ratio R(mu Au -> e Au) (90% CL upper limit)
current_bound.reference: Bertl et al. (SINDRUM II), 'A search for muon to electron conversion in muonic gold', EPJC 47, 337 (2006)

what_it_excludes: Complementary to mu -> e gamma: mu-e conversion is sensitive to scalar/vector four-fermion CLFV operators that do not generate mu -> e gamma at one loop. Together they probe orthogonal directions in BSM CLFV parameter space. Mu2e/COMET will close many SUSY and leptoquark models if they see no signal.

parameterization.name: SMEFT CLFV dipole + scalar/vector four-fermion operators: C_egamma^(12), C_lq^(1)/(3), C_eu, C_ed (mu-e off-diagonal).
parameterization.reference: Cirigliano, Davidson, Kuno, PLB 771, 242 (2017), arXiv:1702.03020; Kitano, Koike, Okada, PRD 66, 096002 (2002).
parameterization.summary: Conversion rate is a coherent sum over nucleons; nuclear-structure-dependent overlap integrals tabulated in Kitano-Koike-Okada.

procedural.predicted_observable: R(mu N -> e N) = sum |a_dipole + a_scalar + a_vector|^2 with target-dependent coefficients computed from nuclear wavefunctions.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Operator-dependent; for pure dipole, |C_egamma^(12) / Lambda^2| < ~few x 10^-13 GeV^-2 (similar to MEG).
framework_inputs: Match the framework onto SMEFT CLFV operators including four-fermion ones.
Use Kitano-Koike-Okada overlap integrals (Au, Al, Ti) to compute the conversion rate per target.

primary_references.citation: Bertl et al. (SINDRUM II), 'A search for muon to electron conversion in muonic gold', EPJC 47, 337 (2006)
Kitano, Koike, Okada, 'Detailed calculation of lepton flavor violating muon-electron conversion rate for various nuclei', PRD 66, 096002 (2002); Erratum PRD 76, 059902 (2007)
Mu2e Collaboration, 'Mu2e Technical Design Report', arXiv:1501.05241 (2015)
